When you're trying to solve a bug, and the process is slow and takes a long time, often it's because you're confused, and some thing that you thought was true was actually not true, and it can take a long time to figure that out. Assertions reduce this kind of situation drastically, both in the evolution of the code (because things you thought were true, but weren't, get weeded out and fixed early) and during the debugging session (you can look at asserts and see textual reminders of what is known to be true right now).
